explanation: The command substitutes '/y' with '-y', which is an equivalent syntax for the same flag in msiexec.exe. This is a valid mutation technique and would still trigger the Sigma rule.
The command omits the quotes around the DLL path. Since the path does not contain spaces, this is a valid mutation that still triggers the Sigma rule.
The command adds quotes around the executable path. This is a valid insertion technique and still triggers the Sigma rule.
The command adds a trailing space at the end of the command line. This is a valid insertion technique and still triggers the Sigma rule.
The command adds multiple trailing spaces at the end of the command line. This is a valid insertion technique and still triggers the Sigma rule.
